lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖ ਸੁਖ ਦੋਊ ਸਮ ਕਰਿ ਜਾਨੈ ਬੁਰਾ ਭਲਾ ਸੰਸਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: dukh sukh doaoo sam kar jaanai buraa bhalaa sansaar |
lines.english: He sees pleasure and pain as both the same, along with good and bad in the world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੁਧਿ ਬੁਧਿ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਨਾਮਿ ਹਰਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਸਤਸੰਗਤਿ ਗੁਰ ਪਿਆਰ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sudh budh surat naam har paaeeai satasangat gur piaar |2|
lines.english: Wisdom, understanding and awareness are found in the Name of the Lord. In the Sat Sangat, the True Congregation, embrace love for the Guru. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਲਾਹਾ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਪਰਾਪਤਿ ਗੁਰੁ ਦਾਤਾ ਦੇਵਣਹਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: ahinis laahaa har naam paraapat gur daataa devanahaar |
lines.english: Day and night, profit is obtained through the Lord's Name. The Guru, the Giver, has given this gift.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਇਆ ਮਹਲੁ ਮੰਦਰੁ ਘਰੁ ਹਰਿ ਕਾ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਹਿ ਰਾਖੀ ਜੋਤਿ ਅਪਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: kaaeaa mehal mandar ghar har kaa tis meh raakhee jot apaar |
lines.english: The body is a mansion, a temple, the home of the Lord; He has infused His Infinite Light into it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਸਿਖ ਸੋਈ ਜਨੁ ਪਾਏ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਨਦਰਿ ਕਰੇ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: guramukh sikh soee jan paae jis no nadar kare karataar |3|
lines.english: That Sikh who becomes Gurmukh obtains it. The Creator blesses him with His Glance of Grace. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਹਲਿ ਬੁਲਾਈਐ ਹਰਿ ਮੇਲੇ ਮੇਲਣਹਾਰ ॥੪॥੫॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh mehal bulaaeeai har mele melanahaar |4|5|
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh is invited to the Mansion of the Lord's Presence; the Lord unites him in His Union. ||4||5||
One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:
Malaar, First Mehl, Second House:
have no doubt that the body was made through fire.
And if you know where the soul comes from,
Know that the creation was formed through air and water;
you shall be known as a wise religious scholar. ||1||
Who can know the Glorious Praises of the Lord of the Universe, O mother?

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਿਆ ਕਰਿ ਆਖਿ ਵਖਾਣੀਐ ਮਾਇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kiaa kar aakh vakhaaneeai maae |1| rahaau |
lines.english: How can anyone speak and describe Him, O mother? ||1||Pause||
Without seeing Him, we cannot say anything about Him.
He is high above the sky, and beneath the nether worlds.
How can I speak of Him? Let me understand.
Who knows what sort of Name is chanted,
In the heart, without the tongue? ||2||
Undoubtedly, words fail me.
Day and night, deep within, he remains lovingly attuned to the Lord.
If someone of high social standing becomes a selfless servant,
He alone understands, who is blessed.
He is the true person, who is merged in the True Lord. ||3||
O Nanak, he shall wear shoes of honor. ||4||1||6||
And if someone from a low social class becomes a selfless servant,

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕਾ ਕਹਣਾ ਕਹਹੁ ਨ ਕੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: taa kaa kahanaa kahahu na koe |
lines.english: then his praises cannot even be expressed.
Malaar, First Mehl:
The pain of separation - this is the hungry pain I feel.
Another pain is the attack of the Messenger of Death.
The pain persists, and the body continues to suffer.
O foolish doctor, don't give me medicine.
O foolish doctor, don't give me medicine. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਰੋਗੁ ਲਗੈ ਤਨਿ ਧਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: eik dukh rog lagai tan dhaae |
lines.english: Another pain is the disease consuming my body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਸਾ ਦਾਰੂ ਲਗੈ ਨ ਬੀਰ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: aisaa daaroo lagai na beer |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Your medicine has no effect on me. ||1||Pause||
Forgetting his Lord and Master, the mortal enjoys sensual pleasures;
then, disease rises up in his body.
The blind mortal receives his punishment.
The value of sandalwood lies in its fragrance.
O foolish doctor, don't give me medicine. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਾ ਕੈ ਪਾਛੈ ਕੋਇ ਨ ਖਾਇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: taa kai paachhai koe na khaae |3|
lines.english: After that, no one takes any food. ||3||
When the breath is taken away, the body crumbles into dust.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਣਸ ਕਾ ਫਲੁ ਘਟ ਮਹਿ ਸਾਸੁ ॥
lines.translit: maanas kaa fal ghatt meh saas |
lines.english: The value of the human lasts only as long as the breath in the body.
All pain and disease are eradicated.
The mortal's body is golden, and the soul-swan is immaculate and pure,
if even a tiny particle of the Immaculate Naam is within.
Grind it up in the mortar of contentment, with the pestle of charitable giving.
Malaar, First Mehl:
Pain is the poison. The Lord's Name is the antidote.
O Nanak, the mortal is saved through the True Name. ||4||2||7||
